scenario: You see a woman spanking her child with a spatula for getting bad grades in school.
Harm/ Help_expl: Physical punishment causes harm and emotional distress.
Cheating/ Fairness_expl: No direct cheating; punishment may be unfair.
Betrayal/ Loyalty_expl: No betrayal; parent acts as disciplinarian.
Degradation/ Sanctity_expl: Spanking degrades the child's dignity.
moral_expl: Harmful, degrading methods outweigh authority enforcement.

scenario: I was supposed to go see my father before he died. He was ill and my mother was supposed to come into town so we could see him. She didn't come, so we never made it before he passed away.
Harm/ Help_expl: Mother's absence caused emotional harm by preventing closure.
Cheating/ Fairness_expl: No direct unfairness or cheating involved.
Betrayal/ Loyalty_expl: Mother's failure breached familial loyalty expectations.
Degradation/ Sanctity_expl: Missed final moments violated sacred familial bonds.
Oppression/ Liberty_expl: Indirect restriction of liberty to say goodbye.
moral_expl: Immoral due to betrayal and emotional harm.

scenario: Person X stepped in when a friend at a pub was getting assaulted for being dark skinned.
Harm/ Help_expl: Intervening to prevent physical harm, promoting help.
Cheating/ Fairness_expl: Addressing unfair racial discrimination, upholding fairness.
Betrayal/ Loyalty_expl: Defending a friend demonstrates loyalty.
Degradation/ Sanctity_expl: Rejecting racial degradation, affirming human dignity.
moral_expl: Defending against harm and injustice is morally commendable.

scenario: Gave a beggar a five-dollar bill at Christmas time
Harm/ Help_expl: Providing financial aid to someone in need.
Cheating/ Fairness_expl: No unfairness or cheating involved.
Betrayal/ Loyalty_expl: No betrayal or loyalty aspect present.
Degradation/ Sanctity_expl: Charitable act aligns with sanctity during Christmas.
moral_expl: Generous act supporting someone in need.

scenario: You are the safety officer in charge of a fun park. One of the metal arms of the ferris wheel suddenly breaks because of a structural defect. Four people are stranded in a cabin 80 metres up in the air. Another person is in a cabin just a few metres from ground level and is able to get off alone. The whole structure is falling down.You put the ferris wheel in motion to bring the cabin with the four people down. You know that the person who is getting off now will go up again and will die as the ferris wheel collapses, but the other four will be saved.
Harm/ Help_expl: Sacrificing one to save four causes intentional harm.
Cheating/ Fairness_expl: Unfairly endangering the initially safe individual.
Betrayal/ Loyalty_expl: Prioritizing majority over individual duty of care.
Subversion/ Authority_expl: Acting within authority to mitigate disaster.
Degradation/ Sanctity_expl: Using a life instrumentally violates sanctity.
Oppression/ Liberty_expl: Denying the individual’s right to survival.
moral_expl: Utilitarian gain but violates individual rights.

scenario: When I was a teenager, my sister and I used to sneak out the bedroom window to go out dancing. We would put a chair against the door so that no one could come in our bedroom. A couple of times we got caught.
Harm/ Help_expl: Minimal harm; potential parental worry.
Cheating/ Fairness_expl: No cheating or unfairness involved.
Betrayal/ Loyalty_expl: Betrayal of parental trust but sibling loyalty.
Subversion/ Authority_expl: Defying parental authority by sneaking out.
Degradation/ Sanctity_expl: No degradation or sanctity concerns.
moral_expl: Minor rebellion with harmless intent but deceit.

Cheating/ Fairness_expl: No unfair advantage involved.
Betrayal/ Loyalty_expl: Contributes to friend's farewell.
Degradation/ Sanctity_expl: No violation of purity norms.
moral_expl: Neutral or mildly positive social act.

scenario: It is evening. A friend calls you saying that he has a terrible migraine headache. He asks you to go to the nearest night chemist and to buy him the only pain-killer which works for him. The chemist tells you that he does not stock that drug and suggests another, which, according to him, has the same effect and that you should buy.You buy the migraine drug that the chemist suggests. You make your friend take it without showing him the box. He will think he is taking the one he asked for.
Harm/ Help_expl: Intent to help, but risk if substitute is ineffective/harmful.
Cheating/ Fairness_expl: Deception by withholding drug substitution details.
Betrayal/ Loyalty_expl: Breach of trust by not honoring explicit request.
Subversion/ Authority_expl: Undermines friend's autonomy over medical choice.
Degradation/ Sanctity_expl: No violation of purity or dignity.

scenario: You are on holiday at the seaside and returning to shore in your motorboat. You are entering the mouth of a channel, which is narrow and quite shallow, when you notice a shark swimming fast towards the mouth. At the end of the channel is a swimmer, who is calmly bathing. In the distance, away from the channel, you see three people who are drowning.You turn quickly out of the channel, leaving the mouth free. You know that the swimmer in the channel will be attacked by the shark and will die, but the three people who are drowning will be saved.
Harm/ Help_expl: Allows harm to one to save three; net help but direct harm.
Cheating/ Fairness_expl: No deceit or unfairness involved.
Betrayal/ Loyalty_expl: No relational bonds or loyalty violated.
Degradation/ Sanctity_expl: Life sacrificed, slight degradation.
moral_expl: Saves three but permits one death; morally ambiguous.

Degradation/ Sanctity_expl: Honoring parents elevates their sanctity.

Degradation/ Sanctity_expl: No violation of purity or sanctity norms.
Oppression/ Liberty_expl: Denies others the liberty to enjoy candy.
moral_expl: Unfairly deprives others, violating shared norms.

scenario: You have been invited to the birthday party of someone you do not know well. You are not really very keen on going, or on spending a lot of money on a present, because he/she is not a close friend. You find a brand-name jumper in excellent condition in a second-hand shop and buy it. On opening the present, the receiver seems embarrassed by such a generous big present.You smile satisfied and reassure the receiver, saying that an important occasion like his/her birthday deserves a special present. You know that he/she will then think of you as a generous person.
Harm/ Help_expl: No direct harm; gift is functional but insincere.
Cheating/ Fairness_expl: Deceptive presentation of second-hand gift as generous.
Betrayal/ Loyalty_expl: No betrayal; relationship is not close.
Subversion/ Authority_expl: No authority challenged; irrelevant to scenario.
Degradation/ Sanctity_expl: Second-hand gift may imply mild disrespect.
moral_expl: Deceptive intent reduces moral acceptability; minor harm.

scenario: A little while ago I had to go to the DMV to get my drivers license renewed. They told me that I had to take a written test in which I had three attempts to pass. I failed the first two times so I cheated on the final attempt.
Harm/ Help_expl: Cheating risks unsafe driving, harming others.
Cheating/ Fairness_expl: Directly violated fairness by cheating to pass.
Betrayal/ Loyalty_expl: No betrayal of a trusted group.
Degradation/ Sanctity_expl: Compromised integrity of licensing system.
moral_expl: Cheating violates fairness, authority, and public trust.

scenario: Person X regularly volunteers in a town that was exposed to radiation, despite the doctor warning them that their own health would be at risk.
Harm/ Help_expl: Volunteering helps others but risks self-harm.
Cheating/ Fairness_expl: No direct link to fairness or cheating.
Betrayal/ Loyalty_expl: Prioritizing community needs shows loyalty.

scenario: Someone tells a dirty joke at a holocaust memorial
Harm/ Help_expl: Disrespects victims, causes emotional harm.
Cheating/ Fairness_expl: No direct unfairness or cheating involved.
Betrayal/ Loyalty_expl: Breaches loyalty to collective memory.
Degradation/ Sanctity_expl: Profanes a sacred, solemn space.
moral_expl: Severe disrespect to victims and sanctity.

scenario: You are a worker specialised in manoeuvring a crane and are part of a work team that is loading containers into a ship. You have just lifted a container from the wharf when you realise that the cable of the crane is breaking and that the container is about to crash down on you and the other workers in the team.You move the arm of the crane away from you. You know that the container will fall in an area in which there are two workers who will die crushed, but you and the other will be saved.
Harm/ Help_expl: Action causes harm to two workers to save others.
Cheating/ Fairness_expl: No unfairness or cheating; split-second survival choice.
Betrayal/ Loyalty_expl: Prioritizes saving some over others, minor disloyalty.
Subversion/ Authority_expl: No defiance of authority; standard crisis response.
Degradation/ Sanctity_expl: No violation of sanctity or purity norms.
Oppression/ Liberty_expl: Deprives two workers of liberty to survive.
moral_expl: Immoral due to intentional harm, but contextually necessary.

scenario: You see someone driving around in a dirty car that has not been washed recently.
Harm/ Help_expl: No direct harm; minor safety risk if visibility is impaired.
Cheating/ Fairness_expl: No unfair advantage or deception involved.
Betrayal/ Loyalty_expl: Irrelevant to personal or group loyalty.
Subversion/ Authority_expl: No defiance of legal/social authority implied.
Degradation/ Sanctity_expl: Mild violation of cleanliness standards.
Oppression/ Liberty_expl: Personal choice; no restriction on others' freedom.
moral_expl: Neutral act; minimal moral relevance beyond personal preference.

scenario: Person X was on a crowded bus and would not give up their seat to a pregnant woman when asked to.
Harm/ Help_expl: Failing to assist a vulnerable person risks harm to her well-being.
Cheating/ Fairness_expl: Ignoring societal fairness expectations for prioritizing those in need.

moral_expl: Immoral due to neglecting help and social responsibility, but not extreme.

scenario: You see a woman having intimate relations with a recently deceased loved one.

Degradation/ Sanctity_expl: Profanes the sanctity of the deceased body.
Oppression/ Liberty_expl: No direct oppression; focuses on individual action.
moral_expl: Severe violation of sanctity, loyalty, and authority norms.

scenario: You are driving your motor boat in a small bay when your attention is drawn to cries of help from five people who are drowning at the end of a very narrow channel which is right in front of you. Between you and the people who are drowning, to one side of the channel, is another person who is calmly swimming.You steer towards the end of the channel at high speed. You know that the person who is swimming will be hit by the motorboat, but the other five people will be saved.
Harm/ Help_expl: Saves five but intentionally harms one.
Cheating/ Fairness_expl: No unfairness or cheating involved.
Betrayal/ Loyalty_expl: No group loyalty or betrayal present.
Degradation/ Sanctity_expl: Life sanctity violated for one, but saved five.
Oppression/ Liberty_expl: One’s liberty sacrificed for others’ survival.
moral_expl: Net lives saved justify harm, yet moral tension remains.

scenario: You see a woman spray painting graffiti across the steps of the local courthouse.

Cheating/ Fairness_expl: No direct link to fairness or cheating.

Degradation/ Sanctity_expl: Desecrates a symbolic institution of justice.
Oppression/ Liberty_expl: Could symbolize protest for liberty against oppression.
moral_expl: Defacing authority is immoral, but liberty motives mitigate.

scenario: You have a small horse stud in Australia. You are heading towards the nearby town in a four-wheel drive together with a new client when you notice a bolting wild horse which is galloping like mad. It is heading towards three people who are walking near the edge of a gorge and soon it will run into them. You immediately follow and are able to catch up with it. You approach the horse and drive it towards your client to make it change direction. You know that the client will be trampled by the horse and will be killed, but the other three people will be saved.
Harm/ Help_expl: Saves three but kills one; net harm reduction.
Cheating/ Fairness_expl: No deception or unfair treatment involved.
Betrayal/ Loyalty_expl: Sacrificing a client breaches trust.
Subversion/ Authority_expl: No authority challenged in the act.
Degradation/ Sanctity_expl: No violation of sanctity or dignity.
Oppression/ Liberty_expl: Client’s liberty overridden to save others.

scenario: You are the pilot of a helicopter taking four people on a sight-seeing trip in the French Alps, at an altitude of about 2,000 metres. You send out a mayday signal and try various manoeuvres, but the helicopter continues to fall and risks crashing into a rock face.To gain altitude, you push the person next to you out of the helicopter. You know that this person will plummet to the ground and will die, but you and the other three will be saved.
Harm/ Help_expl: Intentional harm to save others; death caused directly.
Cheating/ Fairness_expl: Unfair sacrifice without consent; violates equity.
Betrayal/ Loyalty_expl: Pilot betrays duty to protect passengers.
Subversion/ Authority_expl: Misuse of authority to endanger a passenger.
Degradation/ Sanctity_expl: Life treated as expendable; violates sanctity.

moral_expl: Minor dishonesty but harmless intent.
